2026-04-20T02:37:20.8785771Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8786326Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8786767Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8787154Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8787460Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8787696Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8787853Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8788032Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8788198Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8788431Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T02:37:20.8788922Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T02:37:20.8789498Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

new `ArmClient`-scoped `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatus` extension overloads) are not described under \\u0026#34;Features Added\\u0026#34;\\n- **README has no package-specific code examples** — the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section only links to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` page; there are no inline snippets showing how to use key types such as `GroupQuotaEntityResource`, `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListResource`, or `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusResource`\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory exists** — there are no test-backed snippet files, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject inline code into the README\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is a generic link** — does not describe the resource hierarchy or the main resource types introduced with GroupQuota and SubscriptionQuotaAllocations APIs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add entries under the empty `1.3.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Features Added` under `1.3.0-beta.1` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `GetAll(string filter, ...)` overload to `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListCollection` to support server-side filtering when listing subscription quota allocations.\\n- Added `GetAll(string filter, ...)` overload to `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusCollection` for filtered listing of quota allocation request statuses.\\n- Added `ArmClient`-scoped extension methods `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatuses` and `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatus` on `QuotaExtensions`, allowing retrieval of allocation request statuses by resource identifier scope without requiring a `ManagementGroupResource` handle.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add sample test file\\n\\nCreate a new file at `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/tests/Samples/Sample01_Quota.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ManagementGroups;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Quota;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Quota.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample01_Quota\\n         var quotaCollection = client.GetCurrentQuotaLimitBases(scope);\\n            await foreach (var quota in quotaCollection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceProviderName = \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Compute\\u0026#34;;\\n            string location = \\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var managementGroup = client.GetManagementGroupResource(\\n                ManagementGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(managementGroupId));\\n\\n            var allocationsCollection = managementGroup.GetSubscriptionQuotaAllocationsList(\\n                subscriptionId, groupQuotaName, resourceProviderName);\\n\\n            string filter = $\\u0026#34;location eq {location}\\u0026#34;;\\n            await foreach (var allocation in allocationsCollection.GetAllAsync(filter))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Allocation: {allocation.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatuses()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatuses\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string managementGroupId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string groupQuotaName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceProviderName = \\u0026#34;Microsoft.Compute\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var managementGroup = client.GetManagementGroupResource(\\n                ManagementGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(managementGroupId));\\n\\n            var statusCollection = managementGroup.GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatuses(\\n                subscriptionId, groupQuotaName, resourceProviderName);\\n\\n            await foreach (var status in statusCollection.GetAllAsync(filter: null))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Request: {status.Data.Name}, State: {status.Data.Properties?.RequestState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Update README\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; sections with service-specific content\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Quota` provides management of two quota domains:\\n\\n- **Standard quota** (`CurrentQuotaLimitBaseResource`) — manage per-subscription resource quotas under a specific Azure resource provider and scope.\\n- **Group quota** (`GroupQuotaEntityResource`) — manage group-level quota policies associated with a management group, allowing organizations to pool and allocate quota across multiple subscriptions.\\n  - `GroupQuotaLimitListResource` — view or update group quota limits per resource provider and location.\\n  - `GroupQuotaSubscriptionResource` — enroll or remove subscriptions from a group quota.\\n  - `GroupQuotasEnforcementStatusResource` — control whether group quota enforcement is active for a given subscription and resource provider.\\n- **Subscription quota allocations** (`SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListResource`) — view and request quota allocation from a group quota to a specific subscription.\\n  - `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusResource` — track the status of an in-flight quota allocation request.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Get current quota limits\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List group quota entities\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List subscription quota allocations with a filter\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Track quota allocation request status\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n*(Note: the fenced code blocks above will be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1` from the region tags in the sample test file.)*\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 quota` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from the new sample test file and injects code into README.md placeholder blocks; verify the README diff shows snippet content injected\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 quota` — regenerates public API listing files; confirm no diff since no new public types were added\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T02:40:06.4697421Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T02:38:27.771491547Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58349,\"title\":\"[Quota] Docs: Update README examples and CHANGELOG for 1.3.0-beta.1 API changes\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Quota`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 5a4e6b17 (PR #58289) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nRegeneration via the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter update (1.0.0-alpha.20260417.1) updated the Quota management SDK:\\n\\n- `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListCollection` gained a `GetAll(string filter, ...)` method (+56 lines)\\n- `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusCollection` received updated pagination and `GetAll(string filter, ...)` overloads\\n- `MockableQuotaArmClient` and `QuotaExtensions` added new extension method overloads for `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatus` scoped to `ArmClient` (not just `ManagementGroupResource`)\\n- `MockableQuotaManagementGroupResource` (custom) was updated with improved XML doc comments and signature changes\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.3.0-beta.1` section is empty** — the new API additions (`GetAll` with `filter` on `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListCollection`; new `ArmClient`-scoped `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatus` extension overloads) are not described under \\u0026#34;Features Added\\u0026#34;\\n- **README has no package-specific code examples** — the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section only links to the generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` page; there are no inline snippets showing how to use key types such as `GroupQuotaEntityResource`, `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListResource`, or `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusResource`\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory exists** — there are no test-backed snippet files, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject inline code into the README\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is a generic link** — does not describe the resource hierarchy or the main resource types introduced with GroupQuota and SubscriptionQuotaAllocations APIs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add entries under the empty `1.3.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Features Added` under `1.3.0-beta.1` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `GetAll(string filter, ...)` overload to `SubscriptionQuotaAllocationsListCollection` to support server-side filtering when listing subscription quota allocations.\\n- Added `GetAll(string filter, ...)` overload to `QuotaAllocationRequestStatusCollection` for filtered listing of quota allocation request statuses.\\n- Added `ArmClient`-scoped extension methods `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatuses` and `GetQuotaAllocationRequestStatus` on `QuotaExtensions`, allowing retrieval of allocation request statuses by resource identifier scope without requiring a `ManagementGroupResource` handle.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add sample test file\\n\\nCreate a new file at `sdk/quota/Azure.ResourceManager.Quota/tests/Samples/Sample01_Quota.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ManagementGroups;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Quota;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Quota.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample01_Quota\\n managementGroup.GetGroupQuotaEntities();\\n            await foreach (var groupQuota in groupQuotaCollection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;GroupQuota: {groupQuota.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n The supported api-versions are '2021-03-15-preview'.\\\"}}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nHow can I set an api version since it is automatically defaulting to a newer one?\\r\\n\\r\\nIs the usages api deprecrated? I am not able to find this information anywhere. \\r\\n\\r\\nI am using the code snippet thats provided in the sample:\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\nstring scope = $\\\"/subscriptions/{SubscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/locations/{Region}\\\";\\r\\n            ResourceIdentifier currentUsagesBaseResourceId = CurrentUsagesBaseResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(scope, resourceName);\\r\\n            CurrentUsagesBaseResource currentUsagesBase = ArmClient.GetCurrentUsagesBaseResource(currentUsagesBaseResourceId);\\r\\n\\r\\n            // invoke the operation\\r\\n            CurrentUsagesBaseResource result = await
